spec_match.rationale: Wires four real CSS.highlights.set() registrations with the Highlight constructor, and the .doc .quote::highlight(quote) rule that sets only background-color shows the inheritance win for ::highlight() — the color cascades through the highlight chain from .doc::highlight(quote).

interactive.rationale: Four toggle buttons (spell/grammar/todo/quote) plus a clear-all button drive real CSS.highlights.set / .delete calls. The .tag-btn.on per-highlight colour styling matches the visible highlight result.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises layered Highlight registries with ::highlight() inheriting through the highlight chain; page delivers four independent registries and a nested .quote override showing the cascade.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing CSS.highlights via the probe. Doesn't show what happens when two highlights cover the same range with conflicting backgrounds (painting order from the spec), and doesn't expose the Highlight.priority attribute that controls painting order.

references.rationale: see-also lists feature index, sibling concepts, and the css-highlight-api spec — but is missing https://chromestatus.com/feature/5090853643354112 (invariant #3).

design_system.rationale: .tag-btn[data-h=...].on rules set background to accent-rose/blue/amber/emerald with `color: white` (literal, not --bg-ivory). Some combinations like amber + white at 0.78rem font-mono are likely below WCAG AA contrast. Audit each.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also is missing https://chromestatus.com/feature/5090853643354112. Invariant #3 requires it on every page so the recovery script can find the canonical ID.

openQuestions.title: Audit tag-btn.on contrast with literal 'white'
openQuestions.detail: Four .tag-btn rules set background to an accent colour and `color: white` literally. White on accent-amber at 0.78rem is borderline AA. Switch to var(--bg-ivory) and verify each pair against the AA threshold for small text.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate Highlight.priority
openQuestions.detail: When ranges overlap (e.g. spell + grammar on the same word), the painting order is controlled by Highlight.priority. Add a slider per registry that mutates the priority and shows which highlight wins visually. Right now overlapping ranges just blend via color-mix transparency, which obscures the priority mechanism.

openQuestions.title: Add Range vs. selectNodeContents annotation
openQuestions.detail: The page uses range.selectNodeContents(el) to seed each Highlight. Real editors usually want character offsets, not full nodes. A comment in the code listing explaining the offset-based pattern would round out the API surface.

summary: Strong CHA demo, especially the .doc .quote::highlight(quote) override that proves color inherits while background overrides. Two cleanups: add the chromestatus link, switch literal 'white' to --bg-ivory and audit the amber pair.
